title: CASP facts file
description: casp/facts.json — an OPT-IN declaration of claims a project has verified and wants to keep fresh. A cockpit that never scaffolds this file sees no CASP-FACT-* rule at all. CASP proves the freshness of a declared fact (source unchanged, TTL unexpired, method recorded) — never the truth of its value. See docs/what-casp-proves.md.

schema_version.description: The facts.json schema version. Additive fields do not bump it.

id.description: Stable identifier for this fact, referenced by CASP-FACT-004's `casp:fact <id>` marker in derived documents.

value.description: The claimed value, in free text. CASP never parses or compares this field — that would require a model. It exists for humans reading the declaration.

source.description: Repo-relative path to the source this value was derived from, or `external:<label>` for evidence outside the repository (a provider console, a dashboard) — no hash is possible for those, so ttl_days is the only guard.

source_hash.description: sha256 of the source file's content AT THE MOMENT it was verified, formatted `sha256:<hex>`. Required for a repo-relative source (CASP-FACT-002 FAILs a repo source with no hash to compare against); meaningless for `external:` sources.

method.description: How the value was produced or can be reproduced (a command, a query, a console path). Free text, never executed by `casp check` — only `casp fact verify <id>` executes it, and only after an explicit confirmation. Absence or emptiness is CASP-FACT-005 (WARN); a known-bad pattern (see src/traps.ts) is CASP-FACT-006 (FAIL).

verified_at.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) this fact was last verified.

verified_commit.description: The commit (short SHA) HEAD was at when this fact was last verified. Optional context, not gated on.

ttl_days.description: How many days verified_at stays fresh. CASP-FACT-003 WARNs past verified_at + ttl_days, FAILs past double that. Required on every fact — an `external:` source has no hash to fall back on, and this is the only guard it gets.

used_in.description: Repo-relative paths of documents that cite this fact. Each must exist and contain a `<!-- casp:fact <id> --> … <!-- /casp:fact -->` marker (CASP-FACT-004, WARN) — the marker's PRESENCE is checked, never the prose around it, which would require parsing natural language.

traps.description: Project-specific trap substrings, appended to the built-in registry in src/traps.ts. A `method` containing any of these strings FAILs CASP-FACT-006 the same way a built-in trap does — a plain substring match, never a regex, so a hostile facts.json cannot smuggle in a pathological pattern.
